{{Quote|What then will separate me from the gods of Naboo myth?|Palpatine|<ref name="Book of Sith">''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]''</ref>}}
'''Naboo religion''' was [[Wikipedia:Polytheism|polytheistic]], with [[Naboo (people)/Legends|the Naboo]] worshiping many [[Wikipedia:Allegorical|allegorical]] [[Wikipedia:Deity|deities]], such as the [[Goddess of security and safekeeping/Legends|Goddess of Safety]]. The deity of [[Shiraya/Legends|Shiraya]] reflected many aspects common to [[Wikipedia:Moon goddess|lunar worship]] found in many Human cultures.

[[File:Pontifex_Agolerga.png|thumb|150px|[[Pontifex/Legends|Pontifex]] [[Maxiron Agolerga/Legends|Maxiron Agolerga]]]]
Naboo [[Naboo philosophers|ancient philosophers]] were revered and honored as enlightened [[Wikipedia:Guru|gurus]], and with the reverence of family [[Wikipedia:Ancestor worship|ancestors]], formed an important aspect in Naboo religious traditions. Paired statues of semi-legendary or archtypical gurus were prominently displayed in ranks on either side of the steps leading to the [[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|Theed Royal Palace]]. Natural elements also had an important role in their religion, and among them was the symbol of fire; the Naboo believed a dead body had to be cremated within two days to return the life force of the dead to the planet's core. The ashes were then thrown from the bridge between the temple and the [[Livet Tower/Legends|Livet Tower]], and into the [[Solleu River/Legends|Solleu River]] before its plunge over the cliff. The Naboo thought [[Chaos/Legends|Chaos]] was kept unreachable thanks to seven impassable doors, and that is why seven [[Laser gate/Legends|laser gates]] were designed to protect the pit of the [[Plasma Refinery Complex]] on Naboo.

Many devoted holy men and women formed [[Wikipedia:Monastery|monastic communities]] and [[Wikipedia:Monasticism|orders]], such as the [[Brotherhood of Cognizance/Legends|Brotherhood of Cognizance]], which focused on a particular deity or an enlightened guru. These religious orders were governed by a [[Pontifex/Legends|pontifex]] and had a monastic hierarchy typical of many Human religious orders. Often the more [[Wikipedia:Ascetic|ascetic]] orders were found in remote and isolated locations, preferring a life spent in solitude, without worldly distraction and centered on peaceful contemplation.

Religion's rites were performed by priests and priestesses such as [[Maxiron Agolerga/Legends|Maxiron Agolerga]], who presided over [[Wedding of Anakin Skywalker and Padmé Amidala/Legends|the secret wedding ceremony of Padmé Amidala and Anakin Skywalker]].
